[2018-03-06 06:25] mrguy888: @Wiwiweb @Coscott discussion of the question should happen out here.
[2018-03-06 06:27] coscott: To clarify, finding out what secret achievements are, I think is great. The problem is getting accidental spoilers, because you falsely think that you finished a game, or never being able to talk about a game that you finished online because you are  worried that you havent finished it yet
[2018-03-06 06:28] coscott: Which is a property that Braid has
[2018-03-06 06:28] wiwiweb: Braid's stars were really super obscure, yeah
[2018-03-06 06:29] coscott: Like if the braid speed runs had a challenge that said time to get all stars
[2018-03-06 06:30] wiwiweb: I'm torn between thinking "the game should make it clear that you're not done to avoid spoiling yourself" and "hiding obscure secrets is valuable for the few percent who will find it by themselves"
[2018-03-06 06:30] coscott: I would consider braid not to have broken this proposed design rule
[2018-03-06 06:30] wiwiweb: good old "The End......?"
[2018-03-06 06:30] coscott: yeah, that is good too
[2018-03-06 06:31] coscott: as long as it later says "The End."
[2018-03-06 06:32] coscott: Like I think I stopped playing the witness before completing it because I learned from the past that the game designer does not play by my notion of fairness, even though I loved braid otherwise.
[2018-03-06 06:32] coscott: and so I expected to never feel satisfied with having completed the game
[2018-03-06 06:33] wiwiweb: because you were afraid of missing things? or because you thought there would be no real ending
[2018-03-06 06:33] wiwiweb: I haven't played The Witness yet
[2018-03-06 06:34] coscott: because I was afraid of being afraid of missing things
[2018-03-06 06:34] coscott: I was not looking forward to the feeling of having completed it
[2018-03-06 06:34] wiwiweb: Sometimes you're not meant to see everything
[2018-03-06 06:34] wiwiweb: and you can always look up spoiler free guides when you think you're done
[2018-03-06 06:34] mrguy888: The only thing we have to fear is fear of fear of fear itself?
[2018-03-06 06:36] coscott: Looking up any guide in a puzzle game feel so dirty
[2018-03-06 06:36] coscott: its like people who look up how to solve a rubiks cube, and then say they solved it
[2018-03-06 06:37] coscott: guides for the multiplayer part of prismata I think are good, but that is because it doesnt feel like the main product is a puzzle game
[2018-03-06 06:37] wiwiweb: well it's more like "did I miss anything" guides
[2018-03-06 06:38] coscott: yeah, that is the thing that I am saying is worse game design than games that dont require that
[2018-03-06 06:38] wiwiweb: to be honest I think the Braid stars are unfairly obscure too, but I can't think of anything else that was important yet hidden like this in another video game
[2018-03-06 06:38] coscott: well the Lunarch Studios answer to are there hidden achievements was "maybe"
[2018-03-06 06:39] mrguy888: Are there definitely secret unlisted prismata achievments?
[2018-03-06 06:39] coscott: so even if there arent, you dont get to know
[2018-03-06 06:39] coscott: It doesnt matter if there are, because the main problem is that the players cant trust that they finished
[2018-03-06 06:39] coscott: so if there are no hidden things, but the official statement is maybe, that is just as bad

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-03-06) ---
[2018-03-06 21:17] Deleted User (Deleted User): 9th ladder game disappoints
[2018-03-06 21:17] mtanzer: yes units were ridiculous back then
[2018-03-06 21:18] Deleted User (Deleted User): if 307th played back in the day, how come he only became top/pro way after I started playing
[2018-03-06 21:19] wiwiweb: Strong disagree on having ranked unlocked from the start. It's best to keep new players away from the scary lions even if that annoys the 0.1% that enjoys getting mauled
[2018-03-06 21:19] Deleted User (Deleted User): Huh? losing isn't getting mauled though
[2018-03-06 21:19] mtanzer: ranked is not all scary lions when the playerbase is big
[2018-03-06 21:20] mtanzer: presumably new players would get matched with newish players.  but still elyot has said and I agree someone with 10-20 games tends to crush someone on their first or second game
[2018-03-06 21:21] wiwiweb: Losing is a bad feeling for the majority of humans
[2018-03-06 21:21] wiwiweb: Good point about ranked becoming nicer with more players 🤔
[2018-03-06 21:22] mqp: that begs for a loss redistribution mechanism that takes losses from people who dislike losing and gives them to people who don't mind
[2018-03-06 21:22] mqp: i would implement a slider when you queue that says the rating band you want to queue for
[2018-03-06 21:22] mqp: works in chess and go
[2018-03-06 21:23] mtanzer: I think it should be inconvenient for new players to start playing ranked but not impossible.  Like I agree that most people would probably be happier not starting with ranked and we should direct them towards the single-player stuff or bots, but I also much prefer models that let the player choose what they want rather than being told "we've decided this is what's best for you so deal with it"
[2018-03-06 21:23] mtanzer: so that the mquanders of the game can dive right into ranked if that's what they want
[2018-03-06 21:23] Deleted User (Deleted User): what about if they type in a cheatcode
[2018-03-06 21:23] wiwiweb: I think locking ranked behind level 10 is reasonable still, level 10 is "I'm starting to understand which moves are good and bad" level
[2018-03-06 21:24] mqp: this is just presuming a very particular learning style, in which you learn by playing a level-10-quantity of fake games or puzzles which someone designed to try to teach you the things that are important
[2018-03-06 21:24] mtanzer: when I'm trying out a new game I want to see what the actual game is about right away usually.  so if the main mode of the game is locked right when I open it that's a turn-off
[2018-03-06 21:24] mqp: i don't think humans are similar enough that the presumption makes sense
[2018-03-06 21:24] mqp: i learn by playing a small number of games of something and thinking really hard about every decision until i can draw some conclusions
[2018-03-06 21:25] mqp: other people learn by watching videos
[2018-03-06 21:25] wiwiweb: Cheatcode is also something I thought about
[2018-03-06 21:25] mqp: other people learn by making unit designs in discord, i bet strshplyr would be a passable player on the "can compete at the bottom of the ladder" level even though he has played 0 games
[2018-03-06 21:25] mundungu: I dont like the idea of ranked being locked, but just make sure new players are strongly recommended to play other modes first
[2018-03-06 21:25] wiwiweb: Either a cheatcode or a very strongly worded warning like Celeste's assist mode, aka "Hey this isn't how we want you to experience the game"
[2018-03-06 21:26] wiwiweb: but that seems like more effort that it's worth
[2018-03-06 21:26] wiwiweb: infinite jumps, this kinda stuff
[2018-03-06 21:26] wiwiweb: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NInNVEHj_G4
What Makes Celeste's Assist Mode Special | Game Maker's Toolkit
Developer intention can often come at the expense of accessibility and player preference. In this video, I look at some recent games that have found a middle...
[2018-03-06 21:26] mtanzer: I think I'd be happy with it being hidden but possible.  I don't think it needs to be advertised too much as long as the people who really want it can find it
[2018-03-06 21:27] wiwiweb: ^ great video about the assist mode and developer intent vs player freedom
[2018-03-06 21:27] mqp: i just think it's a totally weird kind of paternalism that says "i know so much better than you, the player of my game, who is a normal human that knows their own mind, what you want to do, that when you click a button indicating that you want to do it i can confidently say that you are wrong. you don't really want to do it."
[2018-03-06 21:27] mqp: i don't believe it, i think people who click the button are likely to want to do it
[2018-03-06 21:28] mtanzer: yeah I said pretty much the same thing in different words a few minutes ago, completely agree
[2018-03-06 21:29] wiwiweb: The trick is the separate the new players who do know what they want, from the ones who think they know what they want but would actually have a lesser experience if they did
[2018-03-06 21:30] wiwiweb: developer intent vs player freedom is a good debate
[2018-03-06 21:30] wiwiweb: aka "Should dark souls have an easy mode"
[2018-03-06 21:30] wiwiweb: I think it'd be fine with a strongly worded warning like I said
[2018-03-06 21:31] wiwiweb: like, in a perfect world where it costs 0 development time
[2018-03-06 21:31] http404error: I think that's an accurate framing of the crux of the debate.
[2018-03-06 21:31] mqp: ok, sounds like we agree in substance then
[2018-03-06 21:32] http404error: I unfortunately started playing before XP so I can never have the "truly intended" experience anyways.
[2018-03-06 21:32] wiwiweb: Let me try another angle: Another benefit of locking ranked is to penalize smurfing and to have bans be stronger. That might be something intended
[2018-03-06 21:32] mtanzer: yeah separating people who know what they want from people who think they know but are wrong seems like it's exactly the issue.  tricky one to solve properly
[2018-03-06 21:33] http404error: Discouraging smurfing is a convenient benefit, but I think it should weigh very little compared to the new player experience.
[2018-03-06 21:33] mqp: heh i started playing before XP but then my partner played after XP and was so frustrated that she couldn't see her rating until tier X that she wrote a tool to help download and parse the replay JSON to make it easier to do after every match
[2018-03-06 21:33] mqp: but she did keep playing so it wasn't a total bust
[2018-03-06 21:33] http404error: I doubt any of the decisions made were motivated even primarily by anti-smurfing
[2018-03-06 21:34] wiwiweb: rating visibility is another can of worms 😛
[2018-03-06 21:34] mqp: i feel like it's a related discussion but i agree not the same
[2018-03-06 21:34] wiwiweb: I didn't go through it so I forget how long it takes to get tier X

[2018-03-06 21:35] mqp: it takes somewhere between "a fucking long time" (if you are a person who puts a normal value on their time) and "barely any time" (if you are a person used to grinding games for long hours to get to something)
[2018-03-06 21:35] mqp: i.e. i think it's on the order of 10 hours of games probably if you are reasonably good but not great
[2018-03-06 21:36] mtanzer: the current situation with rating visibility is actually the way I would have unlocking ranked work.  you can find your rating if you really want but it's just inconvenient
[2018-03-06 21:36] wiwiweb: mtanzer, isn't unlocking it the same thing?
[2018-03-06 21:36] wiwiweb: you just play games until you unlock ranked/see your rating
[2018-03-06 21:37] mtanzer: I mean for people who can play ranked but aren't Master
[2018-03-06 21:37] mtanzer: but if you're saying people who haven't unlocked ranked yet don't have a rating I guess that's a different issue I wasn't thinking of
[2018-03-06 21:39] mundungu: getting to tier X is basically an infinite amount of time if you only play masters on ladder
[2018-03-06 21:39] mundungu: at least thats been my experience
[2018-03-06 21:39] wiwiweb: "you can find your rating if you really want but it's just inconvenient"
[2018-03-06 21:40] wiwiweb: I could say "you can unlock ranked if you really want but it's just inconvenient"
[2018-03-06 21:40] mundungu: i do remember climbing to rank 6 or something really fast during kickstarter though
[2018-03-06 21:40] mtanzer: the difference is it's possible to do in almost no time for one but not the other
[2018-03-06 21:40] Deleted User (Deleted User): it's nothing to do with who you play as such, you're gaining more rating from higher players than losing
[2018-03-06 21:41] mundungu: it doesnt really matter since master rank doesnt do anything anyway
[2018-03-06 21:41] mtanzer: if you play ranked but aren't master you can just look up your rating from a replay code.  there used to be a site that did this for you, but regardless you can do it in under a minute.  whereas someone who wants to play ranked but hasn't unlocked it yet might have to spend some hours grinding games
[2018-03-06 21:41] mundungu: except you needed it for golden cup
[2018-03-06 21:42] wiwiweb: oh I see you were referring to the replay trick
[2018-03-06 21:42] mundungu: i personally dont really care, but in terms of how long it takes i have a pretty good idea
[2018-03-06 21:42] wiwiweb: I thought you meant "you can find your rating" = by playing more games and reaching tier X
[2018-03-06 21:42] mtanzer: ah yeah that explains your responses
[2018-03-06 21:43] mtanzer: I guess the looking up rating from replay code thing is obscure enough it's not the first thing people think of.  which is kind of case in point 😄
[2018-03-06 21:43] wiwiweb: I think the best middle ground for everything like this is what's in that video, a hidden option, or a very clear warning
[2018-03-06 21:44] wiwiweb: you could have a /showRating cheat code
[2018-03-06 21:44] mtanzer: yeah any of those options sounds about right to me
[2018-03-06 21:44] wiwiweb: cheat codes carry their implicit "hey this isn't what we designed" warning already, because they're "cheats"
[2018-03-06 21:47] wiwiweb: I think it's fine as it is now, but adding a cheat option for the (in my opinion) very rare case of someone wanting to skip the line would be fine too
[2018-03-06 21:47] awaclus: IIRC I got to tier 8 super fast and tier 9 in a reasonably short amount of time and then I stayed there for 3 years
